title: Java Exception Handling
content:
### Exception Handling in Java

An exception is an event that disrupts the normal flow of program execution.

---

### Exception Hierarchy

```
Throwable
  |-- Error (OutOfMemoryError, StackOverflowError)
  |-- Exception
       |-- IOException, SQLException (checked)
       |-- RuntimeException
            |-- NullPointerException, ArithmeticException (unchecked)
```

---

### Types of Exceptions

| Type | Description | Must Handle? |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| Checked | Verified at compile-time | Yes |
| Unchecked (Runtime) | Occurs at runtime | No |
| Error | Serious JVM issues | No |

---

### Common Exceptions

| Exception | Cause |
|-----------|-------|
| `NullPointerException` | Calling method on null |
| `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` | Invalid array index |
| `ArithmeticException` | Division by zero |
| `IllegalArgumentException` | Invalid argument |
| `NumberFormatException` | Invalid string to number |
| `IOException` | Input/output failure |
| `FileNotFoundException` | File not found |

---

### Checked vs Unchecked

```java
// Checked - must handle
FileReader file = new FileReader("test.txt");  // FileNotFoundException

// Unchecked - may not handle
int result = 10 / 0;  // ArithmeticException
```

> **Key Point:** Checked exceptions must be caught or declared in the method signature with `throws`. Runtime exceptions are programming bugs that should be fixed, not caught.

quiz.question: Which is the root class of all exceptions?
Do checked exceptions require handling at compile time?
